_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: The 2026 Sun Belt Conferece has 14 teams now that Louisiana Tech has joined from Conference USA and Texas State has left to join the Pac 12.  Three storylines define the 2026 season: (1) Can top program James Madison continue their success with new HC Billy Napier?;(2) ;(3) .All G6 conference teams are dealing with the new portal era in their own way, but the reality is players that stand out at this level usually end up on a Power 4 roster the following season. Sun Belt Tier framework: When team_notes give a 3-tier range (e.g., Tier 2-4), that's not a hedge — it's the league norm. The agent should NOT over-anchor on a single 'centering' tier; instead treat the range itself as the projection. A team at Tier 2-4 means 'either it works and they're contending for the title game or it doesn't and they're fighting for a bowl' — both branches are real. Conference USA tier framework (tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record): Tier 1 = Sun Belt title-game favorite, lower tier CFB bowl game contenders ; Tier 2 = title-game contender, lower tier bowl game contenders; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game and bowl game outsider; Tier 4 = bottom of the conference where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be a tremendous achievement. When team_notes give a range, the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside — but per the volatility note above, 3-tier ranges are common here and the range itself should be framed as the projection rather than collapsing to a single tier. The Sun Belt is the only remaining conference that splits teams into Divisions (East and West), and the Championship game is played in the home stadium of the division leader with the best overall conference win percentage.

team_notes.app-state: Tier 2-4 Sun Belt East team. Signed their best recruiting class maybe ever, tops in the Sun Belt this year.
team_notes.james-madison: Tier 1 Sun Belt East team. Lost HC Bob Chesney to UCLA, hired Billy Napier who was fired from Florida.  Napier has a nickname of Sun Belt Billy after his success at Louisiana but failures in Gainesville. Lost QB Alonzo Barnett III to the portal to UCF.   JMU is the favorite to win the East and host the SBC Championship game.
team_notes.louisiana-tech: Tier 3-4 Sun Belt West team. First year in SBC, previously in CUSA.
team_notes.southern-miss: Tier 3-4 Sun Belt West team. Lost HC Charles Huff after one season at So. Miss to Memphis, along with 16 of their key players from 2025 who transferred to Memphis along with Huff.  New HC is Blake Anderson who was sucessful HC at Arkansas St from 2104-2020, but was fired from Utah State after 2025 season after some ugly allegations. This team is a near-total rebuild: only 2 returning starters, one of the thinnest returning-production profiles in FBS.  QB looks to be Ethan Hampton, a former starter at Northern Illinois.Should have a run game with RBs Pittman, Hood, Briggs and 4 OL portals that have starting experience.
team_notes.ul-monroe: Tier 3-4 Sun Belt West team. Last FBS team to publish official roster on July 7th.  QB Armenta returns as the likely starter, can he take the next step in new OC Montalto's spread-RPO offense?  Can RB Chaney get healthy & regain form? As for the DL... new DC Tim Reffett's 3-3-5 should help cut down on needs. This DL has bodies but lots of unknowns right now. LB & Safeties have options and look like the strength of the defense.  Projects to be a bottom Sun Belt team.
